So I've switched to a model where I take at least two months, if not more upfront. And that last month is a down payment from the initial deposit. So basically we keep working along and whenever they cancel, then I don't send the next invoice, but I've already gotten that payment and that cash is in my bank account. So if they try to stop early, you know, if any of this coronavirus stuff happens and the client like tries to drop the retainer on me right away, I've gotten the payment for that last month. So whether they need me to work it or not, or they try to cancel right away or not, that cash is in my bank. So having had problems like that a number of times, I would advise people to, if you're signing retainers now, switch to that model where it's at least first and last month upfront. And then the one challenge is make sure you manage your cash flow so that when they cancel and you don't get that last invoice, but you have to work that last month, you're prepared for it. That's the only thing that you can run into problems, but at least you will never do any work without getting paid at that point. Really smart. Yeah, love that. So smart.
